reasoning: The market assigned a 4% probability to exactly 5 cuts on June 12, prior to the June FOMC meeting. On June 18, the Fed held rates steady for the fourth consecutive meeting in 2025 (keeping the rate at 4.25%-4.5%). The 'dot plot' released at that meeting showed that the majority of policymakers expect only two 25-bps rate cuts for the entire year, with only two officials expecting more than that. With only four scheduled meetings remaining in 2025, reaching exactly 5 cuts (125 bps total) would require a drastic, unexpected economic downturn that forces the Fed to make outsized cuts (e.g., a 50 bps cut at one or more meetings). Furthermore, even if such a downturn occurs, exactly 5 cuts is a very narrow target since the Fed could just as easily cut by 4 or 6+ times. Since the mid-June meeting eliminated a cut and reset expectations significantly lower, the probability of exactly 5 cuts has dropped substantially below the previous 4% baseline. I am assigning a bound of 5% (or lower, per constraint bounds).
